title: StrategyRegisterInput
description: Register a JavaScript strategy (content-addressed; idempotent on same bundle). The required `source` is the `execute` function; supplying `records` and/or `view` upgrades the strategy to a v1.4 self-documenting bundle so `strategy://{id}/view` returns rich state. Bundles without `records` or `view` retain the legacy single-function semantics and the same id-hash they had pre-v1.4.

dry_run.description: If true, simulate the register (validate + sandbox sanity) without persisting. Returns the would-be strategy_id plus any warnings. Default false.

name.description: Human-readable name; UNIQUE among non-deleted strategies.

records.description: Optional records schema for v1.4 bundle. Array of { name, on, capture } specs declaring what to capture from confirmed action effects. Stored as canonical JSON; max 32 KiB total.

source.description: JavaScript source — executed in a sandbox starting Phase 3. Max 256 KiB.

tags.description: Optional tags (max 16 items, each max 64 chars).

view.description: Optional view function JS source. Same sandbox as strategies; max 64 KiB.

RecordSpec.description: v1.4 strategy bundle: one records-capture spec.

capture.description: Map from output field name to a capture expression string. Expressions resolve over `args[N]`, `logs.<Event>[<filter>].<field>`, `tx.hash|block|ts|gas_used`, and `view.<helper>(args)`.

name.description: Lower-case identifier used as the field key in `view(ctx, records)` (e.g. "supply"). Must be unique within the bundle.

on.description: Match clause selecting which confirmed actions trigger this capture. Object with `kind` (e.g. "contractCall", "erc20Transfer", "log") plus kind-specific filters (`target`, `selector`, `token`, `from`, `to`, `address`, `topics`).
